title: orobanche minor f. lutea
intro: Orobanche minor f. lutea, also known as the lesser broomrape, is a parasitic flowering plant that obtains its nutrients by attaching itself to the roots of other plants, typically those in the genus Thymus. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits associated with Orobanche minor f. lutea include its potential to lower blood pressure, reduce inflammation, and improve cardiovascular health. The plant is typically prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture for medicinal purposes, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
